en.extract:
Sultan's cuckoo-dove (Macropygia doreya) is a dove in the genus Macropygia found in the northern Moluccas, Sulawesi to New Guinea and the western Papuan islands. It was one of three new species defined when the slender-billed cuckoo-dove was split up in 2016.


Description
They are typically about 35–37 centimetres (14–15 in) long. The males tend to have a slight rose/green colouration on their nape and neck. Many subspecies have whitish underparts, fine dark barring below and/or a whitish cap.
The call of this dove is a very loud "whoop-a whoop" with some differences depending on the subspecies involved.
Sultan's cuckoo-dove inhabits rainforest, woodland, scrubland and rainforest regrowth areas.
The doves can often be seen in pairs or groups. Its diet consists of berries from both native plants and introduced weed species. They can be nomadic, depending on the supplies of food. They tend to fly short distances and low to the ground with great strength.
Breeding occurs in spring and summer. The nest is a flat platform of sticks and vines, either in a fork of a tree or on top of a low tree. One, creamy white, egg is laid.


Subspecies
M. d. doreya Bonaparte, 1854 -  western New Guinea and western Papuan islands
M. d. balim Rand, 1941 -  Balim Valley, north central New Guinea
M. d. albiceps Bonaparte, 1856 -  northern Moluccas
M. d. atrata Ripley, 1941 - Togian Island
M. d. sanghirensis Salvadori, 1878 - Sangihe Islands and Talaud Islands
M. d. albicapilla Bonaparte, 1854 - Sulawesi
M. d. sedecima Neumann, 1939 - Sula Islands


References


Further reading

'''Sultan's cuckoo-dove''' ('''''Macropygia doreya''''') is a [[dove]] in the genus ''[[Macropygia]]'' found in the northern [[Moluccas]], [[Sulawesi]] to [[New Guinea]] and the western [[Papua (province)|Papuan islands]]. It was one of three new species defined when the [[slender-billed cuckoo-dove]] was split up in 2016.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.worldbirdnames.org/bow/pigeons/|title=IOC World Bird List 6.3|last=|first=|date=2016|website=IOC Species Updates 2016|publisher=|access-date=}}</ref>

==Description==
They are typically about {{convert|35|-|37|cm|in}} long. The males tend to have a slight rose/green colouration on their nape and neck. Many subspecies have whitish underparts, fine dark barring below and/or a whitish cap.

The call of this dove is a very loud "whoop-a whoop" with some differences depending on the subspecies involved.

Sultan's cuckoo-dove inhabits [[rainforest]], woodland, [[scrubland]] and rainforest regrowth areas.

The doves can often be seen in pairs or groups. Its diet consists of [[berry|berries]] from both native plants and introduced [[weed]] species. They can be nomadic, depending on the supplies of food. They tend to fly short distances and low to the ground with great strength.

Breeding occurs in spring and summer. The [[nest]] is a flat platform of sticks and vines, either in a fork of a tree or on top of a low tree. One, creamy white, [[egg (biology)|egg]] is laid.

==Subspecies==
* ''M. d. doreya'' <small>[[Charles Lucien Bonaparte|Bonaparte]], 1854</small> -  western [[New Guinea]] and western [[Papua (province)|Papuan islands]]
* ''M. d. balim'' <small>[[Austin L. Rand|Rand]], 1941</small> -  [[Baliem Valley|Balim Valley]], north central New Guinea
* ''M. d. albiceps'' <small>[[Charles Lucien Bonaparte|Bonaparte]], 1856</small> -  northern [[Maluku Islands|Moluccas]] 
* ''M. d. atrata'' <small>[[Sidney Dillon Ripley|Ripley]], 1941</small> - [[Togian Islands|Togian Island]] 
* ''M. d. sanghirensis'' <small>[[Tommaso Salvadori|Salvadori]], 1878</small> - [[Sangihe Islands]] and [[Talaud Islands]]
* ''M. d. albicapilla'' <small>[[Charles Lucien Bonaparte|Bonaparte]], 1854</small> - [[Sulawesi]]
* ''M. d. sedecima'' <small>[[Oscar Neumann|Neumann]], 1939</small> - [[Sula Islands]]
